topic: A student learns their scholarship application was denied
raw_output:
[story 1]

The email landed in my inbox with the dull thud of something heavy dropping onto a desk. My fingers hovered over the screen, trembling slightly before I finally clicked it open. The words swam before my eyes: "We regret to inform you that your application for the [Scholarship Name] has not been selected for funding." A cold, hollow sensation spread through my chest, making it hard to draw a full breath. I reread the rejection letter three times, each sentence a tiny shard of glass digging into my composure. I pushed back from my chair, the scrape of the wood against the floor sounding deafening in the quiet room. My hands clenched into fists, the knuckles turning white. I walked to the window, staring out at the rain-slicked campus quad, the reflection of the grey sky mirroring the bleakness inside.

[story 2]

I kept replaying the interview in my head, dissecting every answer, searching for the misstep, the moment I faltered. The silence in my dorm room was oppressive, amplifying the frantic rhythm of my heartbeat. I picked up my worn notebook, the pages feeling strangely flimsy beneath my touch. My pen moved automatically, sketching frantic, meaningless lines across the paper—spirals and jagged marks that seemed to mirror the knot tightening in my stomach. Every time I glanced at the pending notification on my phone, a sharp, acidic taste rose in my mouth. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small space between my bed and the desk, needing movement to outrun the stillness that had settled over me like a thick fog.

[story 3]

He sat at the kitchen table, the lukewarm coffee forgotten in his hand. The official notification lay between the sugar bowl and the toaster, stark white against the dark wood. He didn't move for a long time, just staring at the printed lines detailing the denial. A slow, heavy sigh escaped him, a sound that seemed too large for the small apartment. He finally reached out, not to crumple the paper, but to smooth it flat, his movements stiff and deliberate. His jaw tightened, and he ran a hand over his face, the gesture conveying a deep sense of internal pressure. He pushed the paper away, the sudden release of tension leaving him feeling strangely hollowed out, as if the very energy had been drained from him.
